threats.title: Permanent Market Closure DoS via Rounding Underflow in `_applyWithdrawalBatchPayment`
threats.hypothesis_description: When a market's scaleFactor grows large, rounding discrepancies between scaleAmount and mulDiv cause normalizedAmountPaid to exceed availableLiquidity for dust amounts, permanently bricking closeMarket.
threats.investigation_rationale: The closeMarket function forces availableLiquidity to exactly equal remaining debts and subtracts normalizedAmountPaid without satSub. If normalizedAmountPaid > availableLiquidity, it reverts, trapping the borrower.
preconditions: Dust amount of unpaid withdrawals remains in the market

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: scaledAmountBurned = min(scaleAmount(availableLiquidity), owed); normalizedAmountPaid = mulDiv(scaledAmountBurned, scaleFactor, RAY)
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: scaleAmount rounds to nearest, mulDiv rounds down. For specific dust values, normalizedAmountPaid > availableLiquidity.
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker can intentionally leave dust to prevent borrower from closing market, forcing indefinite interest payments.

economic_questions: What is the exact scaleFactor threshold where this underflow becomes possible?
Can an attacker reliably create the exact dust amount required?
validation_requirements: Write a PoC with scaleFactor = 3e27 and availableLiquidity = 4 wei to confirm the underflow.

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Forces borrower to continue paying interest; griefing attack
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Single transaction to set up dust, triggers when borrower calls closeMarket

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Any time before borrower attempts to close market

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Test exact rounding boundaries in MathUtils

threats.title: Block Gas Limit DoS on `closeMarket` via Unpaid Batches Bloat
threats.hypothesis_description: A malicious lender can exploit short withdrawalBatchDuration settings by repeatedly queuing minimum-value withdrawals across multiple blocks, bloating the unpaidBatches array and causing closeMarket to revert due to block gas limits.
threats.investigation_rationale: closeMarket iterates over the entire unpaidBatches array in an unbounded loop. If the array is artificially bloated, the borrower can never close the market.
deployment_assumptions: withdrawalBatchDuration is short enough to allow multiple batches to expire over the market's lifetime
threats.config_evidence: Market deployment parameters allow arbitrary withdrawalBatchDuration

proof_of_signal.pattern: for (uint256 i; i < numBatches; i++) { ... } unbounded loop

preconditions: Attacker has a small amount of deposited funds

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker queues 1 wei withdrawals in separate blocks to create distinct batches
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Griefing attack forces borrower to keep market open and pay interest indefinitely

economic_questions: How many batches are required to hit the block gas limit?
What is the maximum practical number of batches an attacker can create given a specific duration?
validation_requirements: Measure gas cost per iteration of _processUnpaidWithdrawalBatch
Calculate time required to execute the attack based on withdrawalBatchDuration

preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Weeks to months depending on withdrawalBatchDuration

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Unable to close market, trapped paying interest

threats.title: Protocol Fee Update DoS via Single Closed Market
threats.hypothesis_description: The pushProtocolFeeBipsUpdates function iterates over all markets for a template. If any single market has been closed by its borrower, setProtocolFeeBips reverts, causing the entire batch update to fail.
threats.investigation_rationale: This forces the protocol admin to manually paginate around closed markets to update fees, significantly degrading operational capabilities and potentially delaying critical fee updates.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Loop calls market.setProtocolFeeBips() which reverts if market is closed

preconditions: At least one market deployed from the template is closed

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Borrower closes their market (normal operation), inadvertently breaking the factory's batch update
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct profit, but delays fee increases for the protocol

economic_questions: How difficult is it for the admin to bypass this using the paginated function?
validation_requirements: Verify that setProtocolFeeBips reverts on a closed market

potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Lost potential fee revenue during delay

threats.title: Gas Wasting Loop in `closeMarket` for Partially Paid Batches
threats.hypothesis_description: In closeMarket, if availableLiquidity is insufficient to fully pay the first unpaid batch, the batch is partially paid but not removed from the queue. The loop continues to read and process the exact same batch with 0 availableLiquidity for all remaining iterations.
threats.investigation_rationale: This logic error wastes gas and prevents the function from attempting to process subsequent batches, though closeMarket will ultimately revert anyway if pending withdrawals remain.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Loop calls _processUnpaidWithdrawalBatch without checking if availableLiquidity > 0

preconditions: availableLiquidity is insufficient to fully pay the first batch

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Loop continues even when availableLiquidity is depleted

economic_questions: Does this exacerbate the block gas limit DoS?
validation_requirements: Trace the loop execution when availableLiquidity drops to 0

threats.title: Market Insolvency with Fee-on-Transfer Underlying Assets
threats.hypothesis_description: If a market is deployed using a fee-on-transfer token, the deposit function credits the lender with scaledAmount based on the pre-fee amount, but the market contract only receives the post-fee amount, leading to undercollateralization.
threats.investigation_rationale: The protocol uses safeTransferFrom but does not verify the actual balance received. This breaks the fundamental accounting invariant that total assets should cover total claims.
deployment_assumptions: Market deployed with a fee-on-transfer ERC20 token
threats.config_evidence: HooksFactory allows any asset not explicitly blacklisted by ArchController
preconditions: Underlying asset is a fee-on-transfer token

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Credits scaledAmount based on `amount` parameter rather than actual received balance
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker deposits and withdraws, draining more actual assets than they contributed
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker extracts value from other lenders by exploiting the accounting discrepancy

economic_questions: Can the attacker drain the entire market liquidity?
Does the delinquency penalty APR accelerate the insolvency?
validation_requirements: Test deposit and withdrawal with a mock fee-on-transfer token

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Proportional to the fee percentage and market TVL
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Equal to the token fee percentage per cycle
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Multiple blocks (requires waiting for withdrawal batch expiry)

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Requires other lenders to have deposited funds
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Severe insolvency, late withdrawers lose funds

threats.title: Potential Permanent DoS on Market Parameter Updates due to Yul Evaluation Order
threats.hypothesis_description: In HooksConfig.sol, the assembly block uses `or(lt(returndatasize(), 0x40), iszero(call(...)))`. Depending on compiler evaluation order, `returndatasize()` may evaluate before the `call`, reading stale data and causing the function to always revert.
threats.investigation_rationale: If this bug manifests, markets with this hook enabled will never be able to update their APR or reserve ratio, permanently locking these critical economic parameters.
deployment_assumptions: Compiler version/optimizer settings cause left-to-right evaluation in Yul `or` instruction
preconditions: Market uses a hook that implements onSetAnnualInterestAndReserveRatioBips

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: returndatasize() evaluated in the same expression as the call that generates the return data
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Previous external call (e.g., balanceOf) sets returndatasize to 32, causing lt(32, 64) to be true
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Borrower cannot lower reserve ratio to access funds, or cannot adjust APR to market conditions

economic_questions: Does solc 0.8.25 with via_ir=true evaluate this left-to-right or right-to-left?
validation_requirements: Compile and test the specific assembly block to observe evaluation order

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Loss of core market management functionality

threats.title: Protocol Fee Collection Panic during Market Insolvency
threats.hypothesis_description: If a market suffers severe insolvency such that totalAssets < normalizedUnclaimedWithdrawals, the withdrawableProtocolFees calculation will underflow and panic, preventing fee collection and any state updates that call it.
threats.investigation_rationale: While prioritizing lender withdrawals over fees is intended, using an unchecked subtraction that panics degrades UX and blocks functions like collectFees entirely rather than returning 0.

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Unchecked subtraction where the minuend can legitimately be smaller than the subtrahend during insolvency
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Borrower drains funds or token rebases down
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct profit, but prevents protocol from collecting any remaining fees

economic_questions: Does this panic propagate to critical state-modifying functions?
validation_requirements: Verify if collectFees is the only function affected by this panic
